title: Unidentified head-bumping stormtrooper

This '''stormtrooper''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] with the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] during the early part of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was stationed aboard the original [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] as part of the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] guard contingent in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], just prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. He was part of a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|troopers]] dispatched to the gantry office above [[Docking Bay 327/Legends|Docking Bay 327]], where he and the others encountered the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. Shortly afterwards, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|attacked]] and destroyed the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] over the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]].

==Biography==
When [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces discovered an incursion on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]], the Empire learned that a security control center overlooking [[Docking Bay 327/Legends|Docking Bay 327]] had strangely been locked. This [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] was among a [[Squad/Legends|squad]] that had been dispatched to investigate. The squad's leader was eventually able to open the door, but as the group barged in, the head-bumping stormtrooper did not account for his height and the low clearance that the entrance presented.<ref name="anh" /> As a result of his [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|helmet's]] poor field of vision,<ref name="AOTC">In the commentary for the ''Attack of the Clones'' DVD, George Lucas states that Jango Fett knocked his head on the door of the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], because he couldn't see properly out of the helmet, as a homage to the gaffe from ''A New Hope''.</ref> the stormtrooper knocked his head on the door, slowing his advance. Two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], were discovered in the room alongside the bodies of a [[Gantry officer/Legends|gantry officer]] and his [[Aide/Legends|aide]], but they were thought to be Imperial property. The stormtroopers then left the room, directed by the droids to the prison level to chase after the "madmen" who had attacked the officer. A short [[Time/Legends|time]] later, the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] occurred, during which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] launched an attack on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] over the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]], and successfully destroyed the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]].<ref name="anh" />

==Behind the scenes==
The head-bumping stormtrooper first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], ([[1977]]). The actor playing the trooper inadvertently bumped his head on a door frame while entering his scene.

[[Laurie Goode]] recalls bumping his head on set as a Stormtrooper, leading many to believe that he may be the unidentified head-bumping stormtrooper.<ref>{{Blogspot|subdomain=starwarsinterviews1|url=2010/05/laurie-goode-interview-saurin.html|name=Star Wars Interiews|text=Laurie Goode interview}}</ref> [[Michael Leader]] also recalls a similar event, and when the actor died in 2016, at least one media obituary identified him as the stormtrooper actor.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.cnet.com/news/stormtrooper-who-bonked-head-in-original-star-wars-movie-has-died|text=Stormtrooper who bonked head in original Star Wars movie has died|author=Fashingbauer Cooper, Gael|date=2016-08-26|work=CNet|archivedate=20200911125425}}</ref> As a result, the true identity of the stormtrooper remains uncertain.

One of the most visible gaffes from ''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'', the "head-bumping stormtrooper" mistake has been highlighted by Lucas years after the initial release of the film. In [[2002]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], George Lucas paid homage to the scene by having [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] bump his head on the low clearance door of the [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']].<ref name="AOTC" /> In the [[2004]] DVD re-release of the [[original trilogy]], the "head-bumping stormtrooper" bump was enhanced with an obvious sound effect to draw more attention to the gaffe.<ref name="Star Wars Trilogy DVD">[[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|''Star Wars Trilogy'' DVD]]</ref> Although Lucas implies this stormtrooper was a clone of Jango Fett,<ref name="AOTC" /> the stormtrooper did not share Fett's voice or accent when speaking.<ref name="anh" />
